---
description: "Use when writing the README, contributing guide, security policy, pull request template, or other public-facing repository documentation for OpenClinic. Keeps public docs focused on OpenClinic and excludes legacy/internal naming and planning docs."
name: "OpenClinic Public Docs"
applyTo:
	- "README.md"
	- "CONTRIBUTING.md"
	- "SECURITY.md"
	- ".github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md"
---

# OpenClinic Public Documentation

- Present the repository as OpenClinic, a standalone project.
- Do not mention legacy or adjacent product names from earlier prototypes unless the user explicitly asks for that context.
- Never mention, link, or summarize internal planning or recreation documents in public-facing GitHub copy.
- Keep README and GitHub-facing documentation anchored in the current repository: describe the app's capabilities, architecture, platform support, interoperability, and local-first design directly from the code.
- If historical context is genuinely needed, ask before adding it and keep that material separate from the main public-facing narrative.
